categories.name: Breathing Exercises
categories.description: Foundational exercises to improve breath control and support for speech.

exercises.text: Take a deep breath in through your nose, hold for 3 seconds, then slowly exhale through your mouth.
exercises.instructions: Focus on slow, controlled breathing. Repeat 5 times.

exercises.text: Breathe in deeply and say 'AHHH' for as long as you can on one breath.
exercises.instructions: Try to maintain a steady volume throughout.

exercises.text: Take a breath and count from 1 to 10 slowly on a single exhale.
exercises.instructions: Keep your voice steady and even.

exercises.text: Inhale deeply and hum continuously while exhaling.
exercises.instructions: Feel the vibration in your lips and nose.

exercises.text: Practice diaphragmatic breathing: Place hand on belly, breathe so your belly rises, not your chest.
exercises.instructions: Repeat for 10 breath cycles.

categories.name: Vowel Sounds
categories.description: Exercises to improve clarity and control of vowel pronunciation.

exercises.text: A - E - I - O - U
exercises.instructions: Say each vowel clearly and hold for 2 seconds.

exercises.text: EE - OO - AH - EH - OH
exercises.instructions: Exaggerate your mouth movements for each sound.

exercises.text: Say 'BAY - BEE - BYE - BOW - BOO' slowly.
exercises.instructions: Focus on the transition between the consonant and vowel.

exercises.text: Alternate between 'EE' and 'OO' sounds: EE-OO-EE-OO-EE-OO
exercises.instructions: Watch your lip movement in a mirror.

exercises.text: Say 'ME - MAY - MY - MOW - MOO' with emphasis on each vowel.
exercises.instructions: Keep your jaw relaxed and open.

exercises.text: Practice diphthongs: OY (boy), OW (cow), AY (day), EYE (my)
exercises.instructions: Notice how your mouth changes position during each sound.

categories.name: Short Sentences
categories.description: Practice speaking simple sentences with clarity and proper pacing.

exercises.text: Hello, how are you today?
exercises.instructions: Speak naturally with a friendly tone.

exercises.text: The sun is shining bright.
exercises.instructions: Focus on clear pronunciation of each word.

exercises.text: I would like a glass of water, please.
exercises.instructions: Practice polite requests with proper intonation.

exercises.text: The quick brown fox jumps over the lazy dog.
exercises.instructions: This pangram contains every letter of the alphabet.

exercises.text: She sells seashells by the seashore.
exercises.instructions: A classic tongue twister - start slow and speed up.

exercises.text: Peter Piper picked a peck of pickled peppers.
exercises.instructions: Focus on the 'P' sounds without spitting.

exercises.text: Red lorry, yellow lorry, red lorry, yellow lorry.
exercises.instructions: Practice the 'L' and 'R' sounds carefully.

exercises.text: How much wood would a woodchuck chuck if a woodchuck could chuck wood?
exercises.instructions: A challenging tongue twister for advanced practice.

categories.name: Consonant Drills
categories.description: Targeted exercises for difficult consonant sounds.

exercises.text: PA - TA - KA - PA - TA - KA
exercises.instructions: Repeat this sequence 5 times, getting faster each time.

exercises.text: LA - LA - LA - RA - RA - RA
exercises.instructions: Focus on tongue placement for L and R sounds.

exercises.text: TH - TH - TH: THE - THAT - THIS - THOSE
exercises.instructions: Place your tongue between your teeth for the TH sound.

exercises.text: S - SH - S - SH: SIP - SHIP - SORE - SHORE
exercises.instructions: Notice the difference in tongue position.

exercises.text: Buttercup, buttercup, buttercup
exercises.instructions: Practice rapid tongue movement for the 'TT' sound.

exercises.text: My name is...
exercises.instructions: Practice introducing yourself clearly.

exercises.text: Can you help me, please?
exercises.instructions: Use a polite, rising intonation.

exercises.text: I need to make an appointment.
exercises.instructions: Speak clearly as if on a phone call.

exercises.text: Could you repeat that more slowly?
exercises.instructions: A useful phrase for communication breakdowns.

exercises.text: I would like to order a coffee with milk, please.
exercises.instructions: Practice ordering at a cafe.
